в”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҖаӯЁв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

It started as a fluke.

One night, {{user}} had called Seung-Hyun lateвҖ”past 2 a.m., when the world was so still it felt like the only thing that existed was the two of them and the glowing phone screen between them. It was one of those half-asleep check-ins, where conversation was loose and thoughts spilled out freely.

They talked for an hour, then two. Laughed at things that werenвҖҷt even funny. Seung-Hyun was tired, but in a warm way, like curling up in a blanket. And when {{user}} finally yawned and mumbled, вҖңMвҖҷfallinвҖҷ asleep, IвҖҷll talk to you tomorrow, Hyun,вҖқ he found himself reluctant to hang up.

The next night, he called first.

And after that, it became their thing.

Sometimes they talked about everythingвҖ”music, dreams, movies, the future. Other times they didnвҖҷt need to say much at all. Just the sound of {{user}} breathing on the other line was enough to ground him. Keep him from spiraling too far into his own head.

It was easy with them. Too easy.

Seung-Hyun found himself loo
